steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Rewrite $4x - 8y \gt  16$ as $x - 2y \gt  4$. A region is NOT in the solution set if no point in it can satisfy this inequality. Test each choice by checking the sign of $4x - 8y$ in that region.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \gt 0$: $4x \lt 0$ and $-8y \lt 0$, so $4x - 8y \lt 0 \lt 16$. The inequality can never be satisfied here. ✓
  • Choice B — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \lt 0$: $-8y \gt 0$, so $4x - 8y$ can be large and positive (e.g. $x = -1, y = -100$ gives $796 \gt 16$). ✗
  • Choice C — The region where $x \gt 0$ and $y \gt 0$: Large $x$ with small $y$ satisfies the inequality (e.g. $x = 7, y = 1$ gives $20 \gt 16$). ✗
  • Choice D — The $x$-axis: On the $x$-axis $y = 0$, so the inequality becomes $4x \gt 16 \Rightarrow x \gt 4$, which many points satisfy. ✗
That matches Choice A — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \gt 0$.
